_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates Roanoke-Valley batch (state tier): Terry Austin (HD-37, R), Sam Rasoul (HD-38, D), Will Davis (HD-39, R), Joe McNamara (HD-40, R), Lily Franklin (HD-41, D). Evidence from authoritative Virginia LIS roll-call records (2025 session 20251 / 2026 session 20261) + ReproRising 2025 scorecard + Ballotpedia/VPAP. KEY DEVIATION: McNamara (R) voted YES on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment repeatedly (HJ9/SJ249 2025; HJ3/SJ3/HB612/SB311 2026). Austin's 2026 second-reference amendment votes were NOT-RECORDED ('X'), so only his 2025 first-reference NOs are scored (no false YES attribution). Franklin is a freshman seated 2026-01-14 with no roll-call record yet; scored from documented campaign positions + ReproRising endorsement only.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND (no public denomination stated on official bio/Ballotpedia)
profile.background: Virginia House of Delegates, District 37 (Botetourt/Roanoke area); first elected 2014 (old District 19), seat renumbered to HD-37 under 2021 redistricting (current term began Jan 2024). Electrician, owner of Austin Electrical Construction; former Botetourt County Board of Supervisors.

terry-austin.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-37 (Botetourt/Roanoke); sitting since 2014. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier; LIS roll-call records] Consistent conservative HARD record on the 2025 first-reference constitutional amendments: voted NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1/SJ247) and NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ9/SJ249); graded F/'Extremist' by ReproRising 2025. Voted NO on the entire 2025 Democratic gun-control slate (assault-weapons/large-capacity-magazine ban HB1607, 5-day waiting period, secure-storage, prohibited-person-transfer bills). NOTE: on the 2026 SECOND-reference amendments (repro HJ1, marriage HJ3, voting HJ2) his vote was NOT RECORDED ('X' / absent) — NOT a YES; only his 2025 first-reference NOs are scored. Election-integrity specifics (paper ballots, photo ID, mail-in), border, and constitutional-carry left null (no on-point roll call retrieved — voting NO on AVR/felon-restoration was not mapped to the specific rubric cells).

0.note: Voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1, SJ247; 2025 first reference); graded F/'Extremist' by ReproRising 2025 — affirms a pro-life voting record.
Voted NO on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ9; SJ249) at the 2025 first reference — declined to repeal the one-man/one-woman provision.

1.note: Voted NO to enshrine same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ9/SJ249, 2025 first reference).

Voted NO on the 2025 Democratic gun-control slate: assault-weapons/large-capacity-magazine ban (HB1607), 5-day waiting period (HB2631), secure-storage and prohibited-person-transfer bills — opposes magazine limits and 'assault weapons' bans.

profile.religion: Muslim (widely reported as the first Muslim elected to the Virginia General Assembly)
profile.background: Virginia House of Delegates, District 38 (Roanoke City); first elected in a Jan 2014 special election (old District 11), seat renumbered to HD-38 under 2021 redistricting; re-elected Nov 2025. Chairs the House Education Committee; also Appropriations and Public Safety. Son of Palestinian immigrants; small-business / nonprofit-healthcare background; Roanoke College BBA. Ran for Lt. Governor in 2021.

sam-rasoul.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-38 (Roanoke City); sitting since 2014; chairs House Education. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier; LIS roll-call records] Uniformly progressive HARD record: voted YES on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1, both 2025 & 2026) and voted to KILL the born-alive-infant protection bill (HB531); voted YES on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment every reference (HJ9 2025, HJ3 2026) and voted to KILL 'Sage's Law' (HB532 parental-notification gender bill); voted the full Democratic gun-control slate (assault-weapons/magazine ban, red-flag/substantial-risk orders, 5-day waiting period, storage mandates); led voting-access expansion (automatic voter registration, felon rights restoration, expanded absentee); voted YES on 2026 bills RESTRICTING local cooperation with federal immigration enforcement (HB1441/SB783) and to raise the minimum wage. Notable cross-expectation (not scored on the Christian-specific cells): voted FOR several religious-institution tax/zoning accommodations (HB2302, HB1279, HB131) — faith-neutral land-use/tax policy, not a Christian-conscience carve-out, so christian_liberty left null per scoring discipline.

0.note: Voted YES on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1, 2025 & 2026) and voted to pass-by-indefinitely (kill) the born-alive-infant protection bill (HB531, 2026) — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted the full pro-abortion-access slate (contraception mandates, repro constitutional amendment); opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.

0.note: Voted YES on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ9 2025; HJ3 2026) — repeals the one-man/one-woman provision.

1.note: Voted YES to codify same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Voted to lay on the table (kill) 'Sage's Law' (HB532, 2026), the minor-student gender-incongruence parental-notification bill.

0.note: No support for private-school choice/ESAs found; sided against home-instruction deregulation (tabled HB2304/HB2551 loosening evidence requirements). Public-school-centered.

1.note: Voted to kill 'Sage's Law' (HB532) — opposed gender-related parental notification.

2.note: Voted YES to raise the minimum wage (HB1/SB1, farm-worker minimum) and expand spending; voted NO on tax-cut sunsets — opposite of fiscal-restraint/balanced-budget posture.

1.note: Led voting-access expansion (automatic voter registration HB319/SB658; felon rights restoration HB964/SB162) — opposite of photo-ID/citizenship-verification restriction.

2.note: Voted to expand absentee/early voting (satellite offices, Sunday voting, ballot-cure) rather than single-day in-person voting.
Voted YES on HB1441/SB783 (2026) restricting local law-enforcement cooperation with federal immigration authorities — supports sanctuary-type policy.

1.note: Voted YES on the full gun-control package: assault-firearms/large-capacity-magazine ban (HB1607), red-flag/substantial-risk-order expansion (HB901/SB495), 5-day waiting period (HB2631/SB891), storage mandates.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND (Regent University J.D. noted but no stated denomination on official bio)
profile.background: Virginia House of Delegates, District 39 (Franklin County + part of Roanoke County); first elected Nov 2023, took office Jan 2024, re-elected Nov 2025. Attorney with his own firm; Bridgewater College (B.A. Economics), Regent University (J.D.). Serves on Courts of Justice and Finance committees.

will-davis.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-39 (Franklin County/Roanoke); seated Jan 2024, re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier; LIS roll-call records] Consistent conservative HARD record: voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment both years (HJ1/SJ247 2025, HJ1/SJ1 2026); graded F/'Extremist' by ReproRising 2025. Voted NO on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment every reference — 2026 HJ3 confirmed against the full name roll call as 'N' (his summary record's 'X' was an endpoint artifact). Voted NO on the 2025-26 gun-control slate (assault-weapons/magazine ban HB1607, red-flag/substantial-risk orders, waiting period). Chief-patroned tough-on-crime bills: HB2015 (mandatory-minimum for repeat firearms offenses) and HB2310 (penalty for exposing genitals to a child, became law); chief-patroned HJ457 (motor-vehicle personal-property-tax-exemption amendment). Voted NO on the felon-voting/voter-qualifications amendment and automatic voter registration. Election-cell specifics, border, marriage q2/q4, and christian-liberty left null (no on-point roll call).

0.note: Voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment both years (HJ1/SJ247 2025; HJ1/SJ1 2026); graded F/'Extremist' by ReproRising 2025 — pro-life voting record.
Voted NO on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment every reference (HJ9/SJ249 2025; HJ3/SJ3 2026, confirmed 'N' on the full roll call).

1.note: Voted NO to enshrine same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3 2026 second reference confirmed 'N').

2.note: Chief-patroned HJ457, a constitutional amendment exempting motor vehicles from personal-property tax — tax-relief / limited-government fiscal posture.
Chief-patroned HB2015 (mandatory-minimum sentence for repeat firearms offenses) and HB2310 (penalty for exposing genitals to a child, enacted) — proportional punishment of evildoers.

1.note: Voted NO on the 2025-26 gun-control slate: assault-weapons/magazine ban (HB1607), red-flag/substantial-risk orders, 5-day waiting period — opposes 'assault weapons' bans, magazine limits, and red-flag laws.

profile.background: Virginia House of Delegates, District 40 (Roanoke County / Salem); first elected 2018 (old District 8), seat renumbered to HD-40 under 2021 redistricting; re-elected Nov 2025. CPA and small-business owner (ice-cream parlors); former Roanoke County Board of Supervisors (1998-2009, 2014-2018). Serves on House Finance and Labor & Commerce.

joe-mcnamara.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-40 (Roanoke County/Salem); sitting since 2018; CPA / tax-policy specialist. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier; LIS roll-call records + ReproRising] Conservative HARD record on most pillars WITH ONE KEY DEVIATION: pro-life (NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment both years; ReproRising F/'Extremist'), pro-2A (NO on the full gun-control slate, YES on firearm-safety-device tax credit), strong low-tax fiscal record (chief-patroned a broad 2025 tax-cut package — elevated standard deduction, grocery-tax elimination, market-based corporate sourcing; enacted HB2042 CPA-requirements bill), pro-police (HB1874 protecting LEO personal info; chief-patroned anti-human-trafficking bills HB859/HB2227 and HB2015-type penalties), parental-rights (Patriot Parents endorsement; YES on home-instruction-access HB2598). DEVIATION: voted YES on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment REPEATEDLY across 2025 and 2026 (HJ9, SJ249, HJ3, SJ3, HB612, SB311) — biblical_marriage q0/q1 FALSE. Transgender/biological-sex bills, border, christian-liberty, election cells left null (no clean on-point roll call).

0.note: Voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment both years (HJ1/SJ247 2025; HJ1/SJ1/HB781/SB449 2026); graded F/'Extremist' by ReproRising 2025 — pro-life voting record.
KEY DEVIATION: an (R) who voted YES on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment across two sessions (HJ9/SJ249 2025; HJ3/SJ3 2026) — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted YES to codify same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3 2026; enabling bills HB612/SB311 YES).

0.note: Endorsed by Patriot Parents (parental-rights group); voted YES on HB2598 (home-instruction students' access) — supports homeschool freedom / parental rights.

2.note: CPA; chief-patroned an extensive 2025 tax-relief package (elevated standard deduction HB88/HB1551, grocery/hygiene sales-tax elimination HB540/HB2006, market-based corporate sourcing) — limited-taxation fiscal posture.

0.note: Voted YES on HB1874 protecting law-enforcement officers' personal information — backed law enforcement.

2.note: Chief-patroned HB2015 (increased mandatory minimum for repeat firearms offenses) and anti-human-trafficking bills HB859/HB2227 — proportional punishment of evildoers.

1.note: Voted NO on the 2025 Democratic gun-control slate (assault-weapons/magazine ban HB1607, secure-storage, locking-device, prohibited-person transfers); YES on the firearm-safety-device tax credit (HB2595) — opposes magazine limits and 'assault weapons' bans.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND (not stated on Ballotpedia/Wikipedia; did not complete a candidate survey)
profile.background: Virginia House of Delegates, District 41 (Blacksburg / Montgomery County / Roanoke area); FLIPPED the seat from Republican incumbent Chris Obenshain on Nov 4-5, 2025 (53.3%-46.6%) and assumed office January 14, 2026 (lost the same seat to Obenshain by 183 votes in 2023). Public high-school math teacher; Longwood University B.A. (2015); former legislative aide / chief of staff. FRESHMAN — no roll-call record yet; scored below from documented campaign positions + endorsements only.

lily-franklin.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-41 (Blacksburg/Montgomery County); freshman, assumed office 2026-01-14 (flipped the seat from R incumbent Chris Obenshain). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] NO ROLL-CALL RECORD YET — the cells below are scored from HARD documented campaign positions and endorsements, not votes, and should be upgraded once her first-session votes are recorded. Documented: 'in full support of reproductive rights,' supports a state constitutional abortion-rights amendment, and was endorsed by REPRO Rising Virginia (2025) — sanctity_of_life q0 FALSE. As a public-school teacher she opposes school vouchers ('money taken from public schools') — family_child_sovereignty q0 FALSE. On guns she nodded to 'support for the Second Amendment' but framed it with 'weapons of war don't belong in our classrooms and on our streets' — net pro-restriction — self_defense q1 FALSE. All other dimensions: NO EVIDENCE FOUND (no documented personal position) — left null, do not infer.

0.note: 'In full support of reproductive rights'; supports a state constitutional amendment protecting abortion access; endorsed by REPRO Rising Virginia (2025) — does not affirm personhood from conception. (Campaign position/endorsement; no vote yet.)
Public-school teacher; opposes school voucher systems as 'money taken from public schools' and 'not even feasible' — does not support universal school choice. (Campaign position; no vote yet.)

1.note: Stated 'support for the Second Amendment' but paired it with 'weapons of war don't belong in our classrooms and on our streets' — net pro-restriction on 'assault weapons.' (Campaign position; no vote yet.)
